div.head:
EXHIBIT V.
Annex 5.

p.hi: Bark Cape Horn Pigeon—Owners’ outward account, 1891, inward account, and interest account to February 10, 1891, with J. and W. R. Wing.

row.cell: By sale goods at San Francisco, Cal.:
For goods left over at San Francisco, transferred to account outward, 1892:
By balance to debit owners’ account
To 1 box whale craft at San Francisco
To goods sent to San Francisco, Cal.:
Paid W. H. Crook, boat davit
W. Bayliss, beef, pork, and hams
T. M. Hart & Co., ducks, etc
J. and W. R. Wing & Co., clothing, etc.
N. B. Cordage Company, cordage, etc
Ticket and expense, first mate, to California
Paid expenses at San Francisco in refitting vessel:
Wright, Bame & Co., towlines, oars, cordage, chandlery, etc
½ round-trip ticket, captain, to Boston
J. W. Ganin & Co., suction hose, etc
Whittier, Fuller & Co., paints, oils, etc
Clark & Webster, ship carpenters and calkers, on repairs
J. Wigmore & Sons, oak boards, etc
California Cap Company, Ailing bomb lances
S. S. Arnheim, stationery and glasses
O. H. Keys, provisions in stream
Dillon & Co., glasses, charts, and repairs, etc
E. H. Bucknam & Co., lanterns, etc
Roth, Blum & Co., beef, bacon, etc
L. Levy, oil clothes and boots
Drawbill C. T. Berse first mate accepted
Paid share Wm. R. Wing expenses to and from California, account ship business
To balance interest account to date

div.closer:
(E. & O. E.)
New Bedford, February 10, 1891.
Case: Cape Horn Pigeon.
Deposition of William R Wing.
Exhibit 6.
(Initialed) W. F. C.
